label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The user is asking about Brad's intention when he shared his own experience with Radha Karnik. The question is trying to find out what Brad wanted Radha to believe by talking about his own development of humor. So the passage starts with Brad saying that improving wit and humor is an ongoing process. He mentions that when he was younger, he wasn't funny, but now he can make people laugh. That suggests that he's saying humor can be developed over time rather than being something inherent. Looking at the options: A) a sense of humour can be developed B) a sense of humour helps impress others C) laughter is the best medicine D) laughter and humour go hand in hand Brad talks about how his sense of humor developed over time, which directly supports option A. He also discusses practicing by observing and laughing at everyday situations, which again points to the idea
